003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.petstore.rest;
018
019import java.security.*;
020import java.util.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.petstore.auth.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.petstore.dto.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.petstore.service.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
032
033/**
034 * Secure analogue of {@link PetStoreResource} demonstrating Juneau's {@link BearerTokenGuard} +
035 * {@link StubBearerTokenValidator} pattern.
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Mounted at {@code /petstore-secure/*} and gated by a {@link BearerTokenGuard} that delegates to a
039 * {@link StubBearerTokenValidator}.  Any request lacking a valid {@code Authorization: Bearer <token>} header
040 * receives {@code 401} with a {@code WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="petstore"} challenge.  On success, the
041 * resolved {@link Principal} is stashed on the request and surfaces through {@link Auth @Auth Principal} op
042 * parameters — see {@link #whoami(Principal)}.
043 *
044 * <p>
045 * <b>Why guard rather than chain?</b>  Juneau also ships an {@code AuthFilterChain} for composing multiple
046 * mechanisms (JWT bearer, API key, OAuth, SAML, OIDC).  The chain is fail-open by design — a request that
047 * matches the chain's path pattern but presents no recognised credentials is passed through unchanged so a
048 * downstream guard can decide.  For a single-mechanism demo, the op-level {@code BearerTokenGuard} (fail-closed
049 * by default) is the right surface.  In a real multi-mechanism deployment you'd compose
050 * {@code AuthFilterChain.create(...).append(filterA, "/secure/*").append(filterB, "/secure/*").build()} and
051 * mount it via {@code @Bean AuthFilterChain} (auto-mounted by {@code JettyServerComponent}) or a Spring
052 * {@code FilterRegistrationBean}, then add this guard for fail-closed enforcement.
053 *
054 * <p>
055 * The CRUD surface is intentionally a thin subset of {@link PetStoreResource} — the demo is the auth gate, not
056 * the surface.  If you need richer auth integration (role-based guards, multi-mechanism chains, server-side
057 * OAuth2 / SAML / OIDC-RP), consult the {@code juneau-rest-server-auth-*} modules.
058 *
059 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
060 *    <li class='jc'>{@link BearerTokenGuard}
061 *    <li class='jc'>{@link AuthFilterChain}
062 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/AuthGuards">AuthN Guards</a>
063 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/AuthFilterFramework">AuthN Filter Framework</a>
064 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauPetstoreOverview">juneau-petstore</a>
065 * </ul>
066 */
067@Rest(
068   path="/petstore-secure",
069   title="Petstore (secure)",
070   description="Auth-gated subset of the petstore CRUD surface; bearer-token-protected via AuthFilterChain."
071)
072@SuppressWarnings({
073   "java:S110" // Inheritance depth acceptable for example/demo code
074})
075public class PetSecureResource extends BasicRestServlet {
076
077   private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
078
079   private final transient PetStore store = new PetStore();
080
081   /**
082    * Provides the {@link RestGuardList} that gates every op on this resource with a
083    * {@link BearerTokenGuard} — fail-closed bearer-token enforcement.
084    *
085    * @param bs The bean store (Juneau supplies this automatically).
086    * @return The guard list applied to all ops on this resource.
087    */
088   @Bean
089   public RestGuardList guards(BeanStore bs) {
090      return RestGuardList.create(bs)
091         .append(
092            BearerTokenGuard.create()
093               .realm("petstore")
094               .validator(new StubBearerTokenValidator())
095               .build())
096         .build();
097   }
098
099   /**
100    * Lists all pets.  Accessible only with a valid bearer token.
101    *
102    * @return All pets.
103    */
104   @RestGet("/pets")
105   public Collection<Pet> getPets() {
106      return store.getPets();
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Retrieves a pet by ID.  Accessible only with a valid bearer token.
111    *
112    * @param id The pet ID.
113    * @return The pet.
114    * @throws NotFound If no pet with the given ID exists.
115    */
116   @RestGet("/pets/{id}")
117   public Pet getPet(@Path("id") long id) {
118      var pet = store.getPet(id);
119      if (pet == null)
120         throw new NotFound("Pet not found: id={0}", id);
121      return pet;
122   }
123
124   /**
125    * Returns the name of the authenticated principal — the simplest possible "auth worked" probe.
126    *
127    * @param caller The principal stashed on the request by the {@code AuthFilterChain}.  Never {@code null} —
128    *    if no caller is authenticated the chain rejects with {@code 401} before this op runs.
129    * @return A small map carrying the principal's display name.
130    */
131   @RestGet("/whoami")
132   public Map<String,String> whoami(@Auth Principal caller) {
133      return Map.of("name", caller.getName());
134   }
135}
